data.title: As a developer using non-feature workflows, I can enter plan mode via allowlist bypass
data.description:
## Description

**What**: The PreToolUse hook permits EnterPlanMode without a brainstorm sentinel when the calling context matches an allowlisted skill.
**Why**: Non-feature workflows like /dso:fix-bug, /dso:debug-everything, and /dso:sprint legitimately need plan mode without brainstorm.
**Scope**:
- IN: Allowlist mechanism, detection strategy for calling skill context, verification tests for each allowlisted workflow
- OUT: Core hook/sentinel mechanism (Story 1), feature-intent detection guidance (Story 3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the hook permits EnterPlanMode without a sentinel when the calling context includes an allowlisted skill name (e.g., /dso:fix-bug, /dso:debug-everything, /dso:sprint)
  ← Satisfies: "Existing workflows that legitimately use EnterPlanMode without brainstorm can bypass the gate"
- When this story is complete, invoking EnterPlanMode from each allowlisted workflow without a sentinel confirms no block occurs
  ← Satisfies: "verified by invoking EnterPlanMode from each allowlisted workflow"
- When this story is complete, the allowlist bypass mechanism documents and implements a specific detection strategy for determining the active skill context. PreToolUse hooks receive tool call JSON (tool_name, tool_input) — they do not inherently know which skill triggered EnterPlanMode. The implementation must define the detection approach (e.g., session state file, environment variable, or tool_input inspection)
  ← Satisfies: "explicit override mechanism"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Allowlist must be updated when new skills that call EnterPlanMode are added — document the update process
